display.purpose: Page titles such as the Leads heading.

body.purpose: Standard UI text, table cells, form values.

label.purpose: Uppercase column headers and field labels.

shadows.name: card
shadows.purpose: Resting surfaces: the leads table. Barely-there warm lift.

shadows.name: pop
shadows.purpose: Floating surfaces: modals, the lead-detail slide-over, auth cards.

motion.name: transition-standard
motion.purpose: Color, border, and shadow state changes on interactive elements.

components.name: Call Button
components.description: The headline action. Terracotta primary with a phone glyph; calling owns the accent.

components.name: WhatsApp Button
components.description: Quieter sibling to Call. Bordered WhatsApp green, recognizable by color.
components.html: WA

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Label always above the field. Hairline border, terracotta focus ring, no glow.

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: Dot plus label so status never relies on color alone. Background and border mixed from the status color.

components.name: Nav Item (active)
components.description: Sidebar item. Muted at rest, Terracotta Wash + Terracotta text when active.
components.html: Leads

components.name: Card
components.description: Resting surface: the leads table and panels. Card white on Paper, hairline border, warm card shadow.

narrative.overview: A warm paper logbook seen in good morning light. The whole product is the daily worklist, so the system gets out of the way: warm neutrals that read like good paper, ink that sits softly, and a single terracotta accent reserved for the next action. It is precise where precision earns trust, phone numbers line up in a tabular monospace column, borders are single hairlines, alignment is exact while edges are soft. Warm but precise.
keyCharacteristics: Warm paper neutrals in OKLCH, tinted toward the terracotta hue, never pure white or black.
One restrained accent: terracotta, used only for actions, current selection, and state.
Tabular monospace for every phone number.
Soft corners (8 to 20px), hairline borders, warm-tinted shadows.
Calm at volume: a long call list stays legible and quiet.

rules.name: The One Action Rule
rules.body: Terracotta marks the next thing to do and nothing else. If more than roughly one tenth of a screen is terracotta, something decorative has crept in.

rules.name: The Paper Rule
rules.body: Backgrounds are warm paper, surfaces one step lighter. Never a cool gray, never a pure white card, never #000 text.

rules.name: The Tabular Rule
rules.body: Every phone number renders in Geist Mono with tabular figures. Numbers that do not line up are a bug.

rules.name: The Hairline-First Rule
rules.body: Reach for a 1px Line border or a tonal step before a shadow. Shadows are for things that overlay content, nothing else.

dos: Do keep terracotta for the next action, current selection, and state only.
Do render every phone number in Geist Mono with tabular figures.
Do use warm paper neutrals in OKLCH, tinted toward the terracotta hue.
Do separate with 1px Line hairlines and tonal layering before reaching for a shadow.
Do pair every status color with a dot and a text label.
Do soften corners (8 to 20px) while keeping alignment mathematically exact.
Do hold WCAG AA: white-on-terracotta clears it, focus rings are visible, motion respects prefers-reduced-motion.
donts: Don't ship the generic SaaS template: no cream-and-indigo, no rounded-card grids, no three-equal-feature columns.
Don't drift toward a cluttered enterprise CRM: no wall of menus, no intimidating control panels.
Don't go playful or consumer: no bright bubbles, no mascots, no emoji, no gamification.
Don't add flashy-marketing motion: no hero imagery, no gradient washes, no attention-grabbing animation.
Don't use #000, #fff, or cool grays. Don't use side-stripe (border-left) accents. Don't use gradient text. Don't reach for a modal when an inline panel will do.
